rules.title: pipeline.repo.json descriptor exists
rules.lesson: the descriptor is the one stored intent every cross-check (DESC-03, BR-04, ENV-01, RB-04) hangs off; absence is invited-not-forced (warn), invalidity is DESC-02 (baseline desc.json precedent)
rules.rationale: Profile, branching model, environments, and recovery objective cannot be reliably inferred from the tree; a minimal descriptor is the one stored intent every cross-check consumes. Absence is a warning; invalidity is DESC-02.
rules.fix: Copy the closest config-presets/*.repo.json preset (or templates/pipeline.repo.json) to the repo root and set type, profile, branching_model, default_branch, environments.

rules.title: A present descriptor is schema-valid
rules.source: machinery — baseline-skill rules/desc.json DESC-02 (presence/content severity split)
rules.lesson: every profile gate consumes the descriptor; an invalid one silently demotes the taxonomy to SKIPs while the run stays green. Deterministic, so blocker is lawful. Absent → SKIP (DESC-01 owns absence)
rules.rationale: Every profile gate and cross-check consumes the descriptor; an invalid one silently demotes the whole taxonomy to SKIPs while the run stays green-looking. Present-but-invalid fails loudly; absence stays DESC-01’s warning. A missing descriptor SKIPs here, keeping the blocker⇒deterministic law honest.
rules.fix: Fix the named schema errors, or re-copy a config-presets/*.repo.json preset and re-fill the identity fields.

rules.title: Declared profile matches observed complexity (the inverse-nag)
rules.lesson: the philosophy made structural: complexity you declared you don’t need is overhead to stop paying; a declaration that outruns reality is posture you don’t carry. Locked profile=core (R4) so the mechanic fires at solo
rules.rationale: Complexity you declared you don’t need (staging+prod+gated promotion under solo) is overhead to stop paying; a declaration that outruns reality (critical with no prod gate) is posture you don’t carry. Either direction fires exactly one warning; a legitimate mismatch resolves as a dated deviation judgment.
rules.fix: Reconcile declaration and reality: right-size the descriptor, remove the unjustified tier, or record pipeline jdg new --kind deviation --subject DESC-03 --reason "why the mismatch is deliberate" --review-by <date>.
